title: Ethical Hacker
description: Ethical hackers, also known as white-hat hackers, are cybersecurity professionals who use their hacking skills to identify vulnerabilities in computer systems, networks, and applications with the permission of the organization. They help organizations improve their security posture by finding and fixing weaknesses before malicious actors can exploit them.
content: Ethical hackers simulate real-world cyberattacks to uncover security flaws. They require a deep understanding of networking, operating systems, programming, and security protocols. Certifications like CEH (Certified Ethical Hacker) are highly valued. In India, demand for ethical hackers is growing rapidly due to increasing cyber threats and the digitization of the economy.

financialFeasibility.note: While specialized training and certifications can be expensive, online resources and scholarships make this career accessible to students from middle-class backgrounds. Strong aptitude and dedication are key.

sectorScore.remarks: High demand in both government (defense, law enforcement) and private sectors (IT companies, banks, e-commerce). Private sector offers higher salaries generally.
